context_facts: Problem: Users need a way to visually compare typical AI usage against advanced orchestrated AI workflows to understand differences in efficiency and capabilities.

Success criteria: The application should allow users to input parameters for both typical and advanced AI workflows, visualize the comparisons through interactive charts, and provide insights on performance metrics.
Constraints: Platform: Web; Language: JavaScript/TypeScript; Repo: GitHub; Environment: Browser-based; Time: TBD based on scope.
critic_blockers: Missing detailed specifications for comparison criteria.
Missing detailed specifications for comparison criteria.
researcher_facts: Problem: Users need a way to visually compare typical AI usage against advanced orchestrated AI workflows to understand differences in efficiency and capabilities.

Success criteria: The application should allow users to input parameters for both typical and advanced AI workflows, visualize the comparisons through interactive charts, and provide insights on performance metrics.
Constraints: Platform: Web; Language: JavaScript/TypeScript; Repo: GitHub; Environment: Browser-based; Time: TBD based on scope.

Option A: Use React with D3.js for visualization. (Pros: Strong community support, rich ecosystem; Cons: Steeper learning curve for D3.js; When to choose: If rich interactive visualizations are a priority.)

Option B: Use Vue.js with Chart.js for visualization. (Pros: Easier to learn, simpler integration; Cons: Less flexibility than D3.js; When to choose: If rapid development is prioritized with decent visualizations.)

context_facts: Problem: Users need an intuitive way to visualize the golden ratio in an interactive 3D format, enhancing understanding and engagement.
Success criteria: Users can interact with the 3D model, manipulate the view, and receive accurate visual feedback on the golden ratio's properties.
Constraints: Must run locally in a web environment, utilize modern web technologies (e.g., JavaScript, WebGL), and be accessible across major browsers.
critic_blockers: No maintenance contract context provided for ongoing support and updates.

commissioner_rationale: The project has potential user value in visualizing the golden ratio, but significant gaps in requirements and performance optimizations raise concerns about feasibility and user experience. Without addressing these gaps, the project may not succeed.
commissioner_improvements: Add detailed requirements for user interactions and visual outputs.
Implement performance optimization strategies for 3D rendering.
Draft a maintenance contract for ongoing support and updates.
critic_blockers: No maintenance contract context provided for ongoing support and updates.
researcher_facts: Problem: Users need an intuitive way to visualize the golden ratio in an interactive 3D format, enhancing understanding and engagement.
Success criteria: Users can interact with the 3D model, manipulate the view, and receive accurate visual feedback on the golden ratio's properties.
Constraints: Must run locally in a web environment, utilize modern web technologies (e.g., JavaScript, WebGL), and be accessible across major browsers.
Option A: Use Three.js for 3D rendering (pros: extensive documentation, community support; cons: may require optimization for complex models). Choose this for rapid development and ease of use.
Option B: Use Babylon.js for 3D rendering (pros: powerful features, better performance for complex scenes; cons: steeper learning curve). Choose this for high-performance requirements.

goal: Build a local-first, modern web application for interactive 3D visualization of the golden ratio.
commissioner_rationale: The project has a clear user value in visualizing the golden ratio, but significant gaps in requirements and testing strategy raise concerns about feasibility and user experience. The estimated cost and time suggest a moderate investment with uncertain returns if the outlined issues are not addressed.
commissioner_improvements: Establish measurable state changes for user interactions with the 3D visualization.
Optimize model complexity and rendering techniques to prevent performance issues.
critic_blockers: Missing maintenance contract context for ongoing support and updates.
researcher_facts: Problem: Need to create a web application that allows users to visualize and interact with the concept of the golden ratio in a 3D space.
Success criteria: Users can manipulate the 3D model, view different perspectives, and understand the properties of the golden ratio through visual representation.
Constraints: Must operate as a local-first application, utilizing modern web technologies (JavaScript/TypeScript), with a focus on performance and interactivity.

Option A: Use Three.js for 3D visualization. (Pros: Well-documented, active community, supports complex 3D graphics; Cons: Steeper learning curve for newcomers; Choose when advanced 3D features are needed.)
Option B: Use Babylon.js for 3D visualization. (Pros: User-friendly API, good for beginners, supports physics; Cons: Can be less performant in complex scenes; Choose when ease of use is prioritized.)

goal: Ingest raw syslog data from an ASUS router and produce a Network Intelligence & Optimization Plan.
commissioner_rationale: The plan outlines a basic application to ingest syslog data and generate an optimization plan. However, it lacks clarity regarding platform, language, and specific environmental constraints, which could lead to significant rework. The risks associated with data ingestion and performance must be addressed before proceeding.
commissioner_improvements: Clarify dependencies and ensure all required tools are accessible.
Develop a prototype to validate ingestion and processing functionality before full implementation.
Refine the analysis logic to provide actionable insights that align with user needs.
researcher_facts: Problem: Need to ingest raw syslog data from an ASUS router for analysis.
Success criteria: Successfully collect, parse, and analyze syslog data to generate actionable insights for network optimization.
Constraints: Platform (unknown), Language (unknown), Repo (unknown), Environment (unknown), Time (unknown).

Option A: Use Python with libraries like 'rsyslog' for syslog ingestion and 'pandas' for data analysis. (Pros: Wide community support, extensive libraries; Cons: Performance may vary with large datasets; When to choose: If rapid development and flexibility are needed.)

Option B: Use a dedicated log management tool like ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). (Pros: Scalable, built-in analytics; Cons: Requires setup and maintenance; When to choose: If handling large volumes of data and real-time analytics is essential.)

goal: Build a Windows desktop single-player Tic Tac Toe game using PowerShell 7 + WPF.
commissioner_rationale: The project has significant gaps in requirements and testing strategy, which poses high risks to successful implementation. The MVP phase is feasible, but without addressing these gaps, the project may lead to rework and reduced user satisfaction.
commissioner_improvements: Add acceptance tests that include assertions that can fail based on game state.
researcher_facts: Problem: Develop a single-player Tic Tac Toe game that utilizes PowerShell 7 for scripting and WPF for the user interface.
Success criteria: The game should allow a user to play against a computer opponent, display a graphical interface, validate game rules, and show the game outcome.
Constraints: Platform is Windows; language is PowerShell 7; must use WPF for UI; should be a desktop application; no specific time constraints mentioned.

Option A: Use PowerShell with WPF directly for UI. (Pros: Leverages existing skills in PowerShell; Cons: Limited performance for complex logic; when to choose: for simpler implementations with lower performance needs.)

Option B: Use PowerShell to call a .NET back-end for game logic while WPF handles the UI. (Pros: Better separation of concerns; allows for more complex game logic; Cons: Increased complexity in communication; when to choose: if game complexity grows beyond simple logic.)

goal: Create a Windows desktop application for a single-player Tic Tac Toe game using PowerShell and WPF.
commissioner_rationale: The project has critical gaps in requirements and testing strategy, particularly the lack of a maintenance contract context and acceptance tests, which poses high risks to successful implementation. While the idea of a Tic Tac Toe game is simple, the execution lacks clarity and robustness.
commissioner_improvements: Add detailed specifications for user interactions and game rules.
Implement a testing strategy that includes both unit tests and integration tests.
critic_blockers: Insufficient input: maintenance contract context is required.
researcher_facts: Problem: Create a single-player Tic Tac Toe game on Windows using PowerShell and WPF.
Success criteria: The application should allow users to play Tic Tac Toe against the computer, provide a user-friendly interface, and handle game state correctly.
Constraints: Development must use PowerShell and WPF; should work on Windows platform; must adhere to standard coding practices for maintainability.
Option A: Use PowerShell with WPF for GUI (pros: native Windows support, familiar to Windows users; cons: limited performance compared to other languages).
Option B: Consider using a .NET language like C# with WPF (pros: better performance, rich libraries for GUI; cons: requires learning curve for PowerShell users).

goal: Investigate the errors in the orchestration script and provide a solution for a web application.
commissioner_rationale: The orchestration script has significant errors impacting deployment, but proposed fixes are clear and necessary. However, the overall feasibility and clarity of outcomes are not fully established, indicating potential rework.
commissioner_improvements: Consider implementing automated testing to streamline future validations.
Ensure comprehensive documentation of the script and its usage to aid in maintenance.
researcher_facts: Problem: Errors in the orchestration script are causing failures in the deployment or operation of a web application.
Success criteria: Identify and rectify all errors in the orchestration script, ensuring successful deployment and operation of the web application.

Constraints: The solution must be compatible with the existing orchestration platform (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker Swarm), written in the programming language used for the script (e.g., YAML, Bash), and should be implemented within a timeframe that aligns with the next deployment cycle.
Option A: Debugging the script manually using logs and error messages (pros: direct insight into issues; cons: time-consuming; when to choose: when errors are not well-documented).

Option B: Using automated testing tools (e.g., CI/CD pipelines) to validate the script before deployment (pros: faster identification of issues; cons: requires upfront setup; when to choose: when frequent changes to the script are expected).
